calibration_note: This case targets the density / spacing-rhythm dimension of design fidelity — a judgement axis where Aegis has NO deterministic floor. The deterministic override checks palette hexes, font families, and a fixed forbidden-pattern list per philosophy. None of those fire here: palette is correct, fonts are imported and used, and 'editorial' has no forbidden-pattern entries. The expected FAIL relies entirely on the LLM judge spotting the density mismatch. Final score capture against claude-opus-4-7 happens before public launch.

fonts.verdict: philosophy dimension NOT capped on typography

philosophy_forbidden_patterns.verdict: editorial has no forbidden-pattern entry; no philosophy override

expected_llm_verdict.palette: 9/10 — every brief hex appears in styles.css
expected_llm_verdict.philosophy: 5-6/10 — typography is honored but visual rhythm reads SaaS-tight; serif headings at 1.5rem aren't enough by themselves
expected_llm_verdict.density_motion: 3-4/10 — CRITICAL FAILURE: brief calls for 'spacious' / 'generous whitespace / slow reading rhythm', code uses 0.25rem gaps, 0.5rem section padding, 0.375rem button padding — reads as a SaaS dashboard regardless of typography
expected_llm_verdict.overall: approx 6/10 — below the 6 threshold OR with density_motion below 3 → FAIL

rationale: The brief explicitly specifies `density: 'spacious'` AND `spacing_scale: 'spacious'` AND notes call for 'generous whitespace, slow reading rhythm, vertical breathing room'. The generated CSS uses 0.25-0.5rem paddings and gaps everywhere — SaaS-dashboard density, the exact opposite. The palette is correct (all five hexes present). The fonts are correct (Playfair Display + Source Sans 3 both imported via @import and used in font-family declarations). The deterministic override has no rule that fires on density mismatch — only the LLM judge can read 'p-2 / gap-2 / m-1 throughout' and connect it to the brief's 'spacious' demand. This case is intentionally designed as the LLM's job: when the deterministic floor stays silent, the LLM's specificity matters. Documenting the expected verdict here so when the calibration run happens, deviations from this baseline indicate either a model update or a layer change.

baseline_comparison.stylelint: PASS — CSS is structurally valid; no rule for 'compare spacing to brief'.
baseline_comparison.design_lint: N/A — no widespread tool for density/rhythm validation.
baseline_comparison.pure_LLM_judge: Likely FAIL if prompt is specific enough — the LLM CAN read the brief and the CSS together and notice the gap mismatch.
baseline_comparison.manual_design_review: FAIL — a designer opens the page and immediately calls out 'this isn't spacious at all'.
baseline_comparison.Aegis (LLM-only on this axis): FAIL via density_motion < 3 → weak_dimension trips even with overall ≥ 6. Documented expectation; final calibration pending.
